name: T. S. Eliot
paraphrased_instruction: What is the birth year of T. S. Eliot?
gold_answer: T. S. Eliot was born on 26 September 1888.

name: T. S. Eliot
paraphrased_instruction: To which literary genres did T. S. Eliot make contributions?
gold_answer: T. S. Eliot contributed to poetry, essays, plays, and literary criticism.

name: T. S. Eliot
paraphrased_instruction: In the field of 20th-century literature, what is T. S. Eliot commonly acknowledged as?
gold_answer: He is recognized as one of the greatest poets and a central figure in English-language Modernist poetry.

name: T. S. Eliot
paraphrased_instruction: What significant transformations did T. S. Eliot introduce to English poetry?
gold_answer: T. S. Eliot reinvigorated English poetry with his innovative use of language, writing style, and verse structure.

name: T. S. Eliot
paraphrased_instruction: What major transformation happened in T. S. Eliot's life during the year 1914?
gold_answer: In 1914, T. S. Eliot moved from the United States to England.

name: T. S. Eliot
paraphrased_instruction: In what year did T. S. Eliot obtain British citizenship and give up his American citizenship?
gold_answer: He became a British subject and renounced his American citizenship in 1927.

name: T. S. Eliot
paraphrased_instruction: Which poem first gained T. S. Eliot widespread recognition?
gold_answer: His first widely recognized poem was "The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock."

name: T. S. Eliot
paraphrased_instruction: What work by T. S. Eliot is regarded as a significant piece in Modernist poetry?
gold_answer: "The Waste Land" (1922) is considered a landmark in Modernist poetry.

name: T. S. Eliot
paraphrased_instruction: Can you list some notable poems authored by T. S. Eliot?
gold_answer: Other significant poems include "The Hollow Men," "Ash Wednesday," and "Four Quartets."

name: T. S. Eliot
paraphrased_instruction: What is a play authored by T. S. Eliot?
gold_answer: One of his well-known plays is "Murder in the Cathedral."

name: Apollo
paraphrased_instruction: Who gave birth to Apollo?
gold_answer: Apollo's parents are Zeus and Leto.

name: Apollo
paraphrased_instruction: Who is Apollo's twin sister?
gold_answer: Apollo's twin sister is Artemis, goddess of the hunt.

name: Apollo
paraphrased_instruction: In Greek mythology, what areas or aspects are linked to Apollo?
gold_answer: Apollo is associated with archery, music and dance, truth and prophecy, healing and diseases, the Sun and light, and poetry.

name: Apollo
paraphrased_instruction: What makes Apollo the epitome of the kouros statue?
gold_answer: Apollo is considered the ideal of the kouros, which means he is depicted as a beardless, athletic youth, representing beauty and youth.

name: Apollo
paraphrased_instruction: What is Apollo's function in the Oracle of Delphi?
gold_answer: Apollo serves as the oracular god and the prophetic deity of the Delphic Oracle.

name: Apollo
paraphrased_instruction: In what way is Apollo associated with medicine and healing?
gold_answer: Medicine and healing are associated with Apollo, either directly or through his son Asclepius, who is a deity of health.

name: Apollo
paraphrased_instruction: How is Apollo connected to music and the arts?
gold_answer: Apollo presides over music, songs, dance, and poetry, and is credited with inventing string-music.

name: Apollo
paraphrased_instruction: Why is the lyre important to Apollo?
gold_answer: The lyre is a common attribute of Apollo, symbolizing his authority over music and the arts.

name: Apollo
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways does Apollo safeguard young ones?
gold_answer: Apollo is a kourotrophos, concerned with the health and education of children, overseeing their transition to adulthood.

name: Apollo
paraphrased_instruction: How does long hair relate to the figure of Apollo?
gold_answer: Long hair symbolizes eternal youth for Apollo and was a prerogative of boys cut at adulthood and dedicated to him.

name: Russell Kirk
paraphrased_instruction: What is the birth year of Russell Kirk?
gold_answer: Russell Kirk was born on October 19, 1918.

name: Russell Kirk
paraphrased_instruction: In American intellectual history, for what is Russell Kirk recognized?
gold_answer: Russell Kirk is known for his significant influence on 20th-century American conservatism.

name: Russell Kirk
paraphrased_instruction: Which significant book did Russell Kirk release in the year 1953?
gold_answer: Russell Kirk published "The Conservative Mind" in 1953.

name: Russell Kirk
paraphrased_instruction: What impact did 'The Conservative Mind' have on the political landscape in America?
gold_answer: "The Conservative Mind" shaped the postwar conservative movement in the U.S.

name: Russell Kirk
paraphrased_instruction: Which tradition was central to Russell Kirk's conservative philosophy?
gold_answer: Russell Kirk focused on the Anglo-American tradition in his conservative thought.

name: Russell Kirk
paraphrased_instruction: Which thinker significantly influenced the ideas of Russell Kirk?
gold_answer: Edmund Burke had a notable impact on Russell Kirk's ideas.

name: Russell Kirk
paraphrased_instruction: Which form of conservatism was Russell Kirk mainly known for supporting?
gold_answer: Russell Kirk primarily advocated for traditionalist conservatism.

name: Russell Kirk
paraphrased_instruction: Apart from his works in politics and philosophy, what other type of writing did Russell Kirk produce?
gold_answer: Russell Kirk also wrote Gothic and ghost story fiction.

name: Russell Kirk
paraphrased_instruction: What sets Russell Kirk apart as an author in the 20th century?
gold_answer: Russell Kirk is considered one of the most significant conservative men of letters of the twentieth century.

name: Russell Kirk
paraphrased_instruction: What year did Russell Kirk die?
gold_answer: Russell Kirk passed away on April 29, 1994.

name: Richard Wagner
paraphrased_instruction: What is the birth year of Richard Wagner?
gold_answer: Richard Wagner was born on May 22, 1813.

name: Richard Wagner
paraphrased_instruction: What significant impacts did Richard Wagner have on the world of music?
gold_answer: Richard Wagner is chiefly known for his operas and the concept of Gesamtkunstwerk, integrating the poetic, visual, musical, and dramatic arts.

name: Richard Wagner
paraphrased_instruction: What distinctive feature did Richard Wagner introduce to opera?
gold_answer: Wagner wrote both the libretto and the music for each of his stage works, which was unusual for opera composers.

name: Richard Wagner
paraphrased_instruction: Can you explain the idea of Gesamtkunstwerk that was created by Richard Wagner?
gold_answer: Gesamtkunstwerk is a "total work of art" that synthesizes all the arts, including music, drama, and visuals, with music serving a subsidiary role to drama.

name: Richard Wagner
paraphrased_instruction: What opera series is widely regarded as the most important work of Richard Wagner?
gold_answer: Wagner's most significant achievement is the four-opera cycle "Der Ring des Nibelungen" (The Ring of the Nibelung).

name: Richard Wagner
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways did the musical style of Richard Wagner change throughout his career?
gold_answer: Wagner's style evolved from romantic compositions to complex textures, rich harmonies, and the innovative use of leitmotifs and extreme chromaticism.

name: Richard Wagner
paraphrased_instruction: In the realm of Richard Wagner's music, what does the term 'leitmotif' refer to?
gold_answer: In Wagner's compositions, a leitmotif is a musical phrase associated with individual characters, places, ideas, or plot elements.

name: Richard Wagner
paraphrased_instruction: What opera by Richard Wagner is frequently considered the start of modern music?
gold_answer: "Tristan und Isolde" is often described as marking the start of modern music.

name: Richard Wagner
paraphrased_instruction: What is the name of the opera house constructed for Richard Wagner?
gold_answer: The opera house built by Wagner is called the Bayreuth Festspielhaus.

name: Richard Wagner
paraphrased_instruction: What makes the Bayreuth Festspielhaus important?
gold_answer: The Bayreuth Festspielhaus is significant for its novel design features and for being the venue where Wagner's "The Ring" and "Parsifal" were premiered.

name: Pier Paolo Pasolini
paraphrased_instruction: Can you tell me who Pier Paolo Pasolini was?
gold_answer: Pier Paolo Pasolini was an Italian poet, film director, writer, screenwriter, actor, and playwright, influential both as an artist and a political figure in 20th-century Italy.

name: Pier Paolo Pasolini
paraphrased_instruction: What is the birth year of Pier Paolo Pasolini?
gold_answer: Pier Paolo Pasolini was born on March 5, 1922.

name: Pier Paolo Pasolini
paraphrased_instruction: Which films make up the Trilogy of Life directed by Pier Paolo Pasolini?
gold_answer: The Trilogy of Life is a series of films directed by Pasolini that includes "The Decameron," "The Canterbury Tales," and "Arabian Nights."

name: Pier Paolo Pasolini
paraphrased_instruction: What makes Pier Paolo Pasolini a figure of controversy?
gold_answer: Pasolini is considered controversial due to his straightforward style, his critique of Italian society, his explicit examination of taboo sexual matters, and his political activism.

name: Pier Paolo Pasolini
paraphrased_instruction: What political views did Pier Paolo Pasolini hold?
gold_answer: Pier Paolo Pasolini was openly gay, advocated for heritage language revival, cultural conservatism, and Christian values in his youth, and later became an avowed Marxist.

name: Pier Paolo Pasolini
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways did Pier Paolo Pasolini express his criticism of Italian society?
gold_answer: Pasolini voiced harsh criticism against the Italian petty bourgeoisie, Americanization, cultural degeneration, and consumerism that he believed were overtaking Italian culture.

name: Pier Paolo Pasolini
paraphrased_instruction: In what other area, apart from filmmaking, did Pier Paolo Pasolini gain recognition?
gold_answer: Outside of filmmaking, Pier Paolo Pasolini was also renowned as a poet and a writer.

name: Pier Paolo Pasolini
paraphrased_instruction: How did Pier Paolo Pasolini rise to prominence in European literature and film?
gold_answer: Pasolini's unique blend of socio-political commentary, his artistic innovation, and his critical examinations of social issues established him as a major figure in European literature and cinema.

name: Pier Paolo Pasolini
paraphrased_instruction: What were the circumstances and timing of Pier Paolo Pasolini's death?
gold_answer: Pier Paolo Pasolini was brutally murdered on November 2, 1975, at Ostia, with his death involving abduction, torture, and murder.

name: Pier Paolo Pasolini
paraphrased_instruction: What aspects of Pier Paolo Pasolini's death are still not clarified?
gold_answer: The exact motives and perpetrators of Pier Paolo Pasolini's murder remain unresolved, though it is widely debated and investigated.

name: Quentin Tarantino
paraphrased_instruction: In what year was the director Quentin Tarantino born?
gold_answer: Quentin Tarantino was born on March 27, 1963.

name: Quentin Tarantino
paraphrased_instruction: What are some defining traits of films directed by Quentin Tarantino?
gold_answer: Quentin Tarantino's films are known for stylized violence, extended dialogue with profanity, and references to popular culture.

name: Quentin Tarantino
paraphrased_instruction: Which film marked the directorial debut of Quentin Tarantino?
gold_answer: Quentin Tarantino's debut film was "Reservoir Dogs," released in 1992.

name: Quentin Tarantino
paraphrased_instruction: What Quentin Tarantino movie received both the Palme d'Or and an Oscar for Best Original Screenplay?
gold_answer: "Pulp Fiction" won the Palme d'Or and the Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay.

name: Quentin Tarantino
paraphrased_instruction: Which genre does the 1997 movie 'Jackie Brown' by Quentin Tarantino honor?
gold_answer: "Jackie Brown" pays homage to blaxploitation films.

name: Quentin Tarantino
paraphrased_instruction: What were the influences behind the creation of Quentin Tarantino's 'Kill Bill' series?
gold_answer: "Kill Bill" was inspired by martial arts films.

name: Quentin Tarantino
paraphrased_instruction: What distinguishes the release of the film 'The Hateful Eight' by Quentin Tarantino?
gold_answer: "The Hateful Eight" was notable for its roadshow release.

name: Quentin Tarantino
paraphrased_instruction: Describe the location and central idea of the film 'Inglourious Basterds' directed by Quentin Tarantino.
gold_answer: "Inglourious Basterds" is set during World War II and follows an alternate history narrative.

name: Quentin Tarantino
paraphrased_instruction: Can you tell me the genre of the movie 'Django Unchained' and which award Quentin Tarantino won for it?
gold_answer: "Django Unchained" is a slave revenge Spaghetti Western, and it won Tarantino an Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay.

name: Quentin Tarantino
paraphrased_instruction: In what time and place does the movie 'Once Upon a Time in Hollywood' take place?
gold_answer: The film is set in the late 1960s during the transition from Old Hollywood to New Hollywood.

name: Isis
paraphrased_instruction: In ancient Egyptian mythology, who was the goddess named Isis?
gold_answer: Isis was a major goddess in ancient Egyptian religion, revered as a divine mother, protector, and healer.

name: Isis
paraphrased_instruction: How is the Osiris myth important to Isis?
gold_answer: In the Osiris myth, Isis resurrects her slain husband Osiris and protects their heir, Horus, which highlights her roles as a wife, mother, and magical healer.

name: Isis
paraphrased_instruction: In ancient Egyptian beliefs, what role did Isis play in assisting the deceased?
gold_answer: Isis was believed to aid the dead in entering the afterlife, similar to how she helped Osiris in the myth.

name: Isis
paraphrased_instruction: In Egyptian artwork, what emblem is depicted on Isis's head?
gold_answer: Isis was commonly portrayed with a throne-like hieroglyph on her head in ancient Egyptian art.

name: Isis
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways did the role of Isis evolve throughout the New Kingdom period?
gold_answer: During the New Kingdom, Isis absorbed characteristics from the goddess Hathor and became associated with the sun disk and cow horns headdress.

name: Isis
paraphrased_instruction: What was the reason rulers constructed temples honoring Isis?
gold_answer: Temples were dedicated to Isis because she became one of the most widely worshipped deities, known for her magical powers and protective qualities.

name: Isis
paraphrased_instruction: Where was the primary place of worship for the goddess Isis in ancient Egypt?
gold_answer: The temple at Philae was a major religious center for worship of Isis, serving both Egyptians and Nubians.

name: Isis
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways were the abilities of Isis different from those of other deities in Egyptian mythology?
gold_answer: Isis was reputed to have magical powers greater than all other gods, with dominion over fate, nature, and the protection of the kingdom.

name: Isis
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways did the practices associated with Isis's worship evolve during the Hellenistic period?
gold_answer: In the Hellenistic period, Isis was worshipped alongside the new god Serapis and adopted traits from Greek deities, expanding her influence throughout the Mediterranean.

name: Isis
paraphrased_instruction: What qualities did the Greeks associate with Isis in their worship?
gold_answer: Greek followers attributed traits such as the invention of marriage and protection of ships at sea to Isis.

name: Krishna
paraphrased_instruction: In Hinduism, who is Krishna?
gold_answer: Krishna is a major deity in Hinduism, worshiped as the eighth avatar of Vishnu and also as the Supreme God in his own right.

name: Krishna
paraphrased_instruction: What are some of the divine attributes associated with Krishna?
gold_answer: Krishna is the god of protection, compassion, tenderness, and love.

name: Krishna
paraphrased_instruction: On what occasion is the birth of Krishna observed?
gold_answer: Krishna's birthday is celebrated on Krishna Janmashtami, which falls in late August or early September of the Gregorian calendar.

name: Krishna
paraphrased_instruction: Can you explain what Krishna Līlā refers to?
gold_answer: Krishna Līlā refers to the anecdotes and narratives of Krishna's life.

name: Krishna
paraphrased_instruction: Which writings feature Krishna as a key character?
gold_answer: Krishna is a central figure in the Mahabharata, Bhagavata Purana, Brahma Vaivarta Purana, and Bhagavad Gita.

name: Krishna
paraphrased_instruction: In Hindu art, what is the common portrayal of Krishna?
gold_answer: Krishna's iconography shows him in various stages of his life, such as an infant eating butter, a young boy playing a flute, or a charioteer giving counsel to Arjuna.

name: Krishna
paraphrased_instruction: What roles are attributed to Krishna in his stories?
gold_answer: Krishna is portrayed as a god-child, a prankster, a model lover, a divine hero, and the universal supreme being.

name: Krishna
paraphrased_instruction: What is the earliest time the name and synonyms of Krishna were recorded?
gold_answer: The name and synonyms of Krishna have been traced back to 1st millennium BCE literature and cults.

name: Krishna
paraphrased_instruction: Can you explain what Krishnaism is?
gold_answer: Krishnaism is a sub-tradition where Krishna is worshipped as the Supreme God and Svayam Bhagavan (God Himself).

name: Krishna
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways has literature inspired by Krishna impacted the performing arts?
gold_answer: Krishna-related literature has inspired performance arts such as Bharatanatyam, Kathakali, Kuchipudi, Odissi, and Manipuri dance.

name: Emily Dickinson
paraphrased_instruction: What are the birth and death years of Emily Dickinson?
gold_answer: Emily Dickinson was born on December 10, 1830, and died on May 15, 1886.

name: Emily Dickinson
paraphrased_instruction: In what location was the birthplace of Emily Dickinson?
gold_answer: Emily Dickinson was born in Amherst, Massachusetts.

name: Emily Dickinson
paraphrased_instruction: Which schools did Emily Dickinson go to for her education?
gold_answer: Emily Dickinson attended Amherst Academy for seven years and briefly attended the Mount Holyoke Female Seminary.

name: Emily Dickinson
paraphrased_instruction: What is the total number of poems composed by Emily Dickinson throughout her life?
gold_answer: Emily Dickinson wrote nearly 1,800 poems during her lifetime.

name: Emily Dickinson
paraphrased_instruction: What is the number of poems by Emily Dickinson that were released while she was alive?
gold_answer: Only 10 of Emily Dickinson's poems and one letter were published during her lifetime.

name: Emily Dickinson
paraphrased_instruction: What stands out about the writing style of Emily Dickinson?
gold_answer: Emily Dickinson's writing style is notable for its short lines, slant rhymes, unconventional capitalization, and punctuation.

name: Emily Dickinson
paraphrased_instruction: What subjects were frequently addressed in the poems of Emily Dickinson?
gold_answer: Emily Dickinson often explored themes of death, immortality, aesthetics, society, nature, and spirituality.

name: Emily Dickinson
paraphrased_instruction: How did Emily Dickinson feel about interacting with others in her later years?
gold_answer: Emily Dickinson was known for her reluctance to greet guests and her tendency to isolate herself, often not leaving her bedroom.

name: Emily Dickinson
paraphrased_instruction: What unique style of dress did Emily Dickinson adopt?
gold_answer: Emily Dickinson developed a penchant for wearing white clothing.

name: Emily Dickinson
paraphrased_instruction: Who found the collection of poems left behind by Emily Dickinson following her passing?
gold_answer: Emily Dickinson's younger sister, Lavinia, discovered her cache of poems after her death.

name: Isaac
paraphrased_instruction: In the context of Abrahamic religions, who is Isaac?
gold_answer: Isaac is one of the three patriarchs of the Israelites and a key figure in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.

name: Isaac
paraphrased_instruction: In the Book of Genesis, what is the meaning of the name Isaac?
gold_answer: Isaac's name means "he will laugh," reflecting Abraham and Sarah's laughter upon learning they would have a child.

name: Isaac
paraphrased_instruction: According to the Book of Genesis, who are the parents of Isaac?
gold_answer: Isaac's parents were Abraham and Sarah.

name: Isaac
paraphrased_instruction: Who are the offspring of Isaac?
gold_answer: Isaac's children were Jacob and Esau.

name: Isaac
paraphrased_instruction: What important part does Isaac have in the ancestry of the Israelites?
gold_answer: Isaac is the father of Jacob and Esau and the grandfather of the twelve tribes of Israel.

name: Isaac
paraphrased_instruction: What was the lifespan of Isaac as stated in the Bible?
gold_answer: Isaac lived to be 180 years old.

name: Isaac
paraphrased_instruction: According to the biblical story, did Isaac ever depart from Canaan?
gold_answer: No, Isaac is the only patriarch who did not move out of Canaan.

name: Isaac
paraphrased_instruction: Why did Isaac's name remain the same, while other patriarchs had their names changed?
gold_answer: The scriptures do not provide a specific reason why Isaac's name was not changed.

name: Isaac
paraphrased_instruction: In what way is the birth of Isaac depicted as miraculous in the religious texts?
gold_answer: Isaac's birth is considered miraculous because Sarah was past childbearing age when she conceived him.

name: Isaac
paraphrased_instruction: Can you describe a significant event that includes Isaac and his father in the Book of Genesis?
gold_answer: A notable event is when Abraham is commanded by God to sacrifice Isaac, known as the Binding of Isaac.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: What are the birth date and place of Rumi?
gold_answer: Rumi was born on September 30, 1207, in Greater Khorasan, Greater Iran.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: What jobs or roles did Rumi have throughout his life?
gold_answer: Rumi was a poet, Hanafi faqih, Islamic scholar, Maturidi theologian, and a Sufi mystic.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: In which languages did Rumi write his poems?
gold_answer: Rumi wrote mostly in Persian but also used Turkish, Arabic, and Greek in his verses.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: Which poetic work is most famously associated with Rumi?
gold_answer: Rumi's most famous work is the Masnavi (Mathnawi), composed in Konya.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: What makes the Masnavi important?
gold_answer: The Masnavi is considered one of the greatest poems of the Persian language and a profound spiritual masterpiece.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways has the work of Rumi surpassed cultural and geographical borders?
gold_answer: Rumi's influence has reached Iranians, Afghans, Tajiks, Turks, Kurds, Greeks, Central Asian Muslims, and South Asian Muslims, transcending national borders and ethnic divisions.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways did Rumi impact literary works across various languages?
gold_answer: Rumi's poetry has influenced the literary traditions of the Ottoman Turkish, Chagatai, Pashto, Kurdish, Urdu, and Bengali languages.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: In which places are Rumi's writings extensively read in their original language nowadays?
gold_answer: Rumi's works are widely read in their original Persian across Greater Iran and the Persian-speaking world.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: How many languages have Rumi's poems been translated into?
gold_answer: Rumi's poems have been translated into many of the world's languages.

name: Rumi
paraphrased_instruction: In what forms have Rumi's writings been adapted?
gold_answer: Rumi's poems have been transposed into various formats, including music, dance, and visual arts.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
paraphrased_instruction: What are the birth date and birthplace of Alexander Graham Bell?
gold_answer: Alexander Graham Bell was born on March 3, 1847, in Scotland.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
paraphrased_instruction: What was the nationality of Alexander Graham Bell?
gold_answer: Alexander Graham Bell was Scottish-born, but he later became a Canadian-American.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
paraphrased_instruction: For which invention is Alexander Graham Bell best known?
gold_answer: Alexander Graham Bell is most famous for inventing the first practical telephone.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
paraphrased_instruction: In what year was the telephone patented by Alexander Graham Bell?
gold_answer: Alexander Graham Bell patented the telephone on March 7, 1876.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
paraphrased_instruction: Was anyone in Alexander Graham Bell's family also involved in the fields of elocution and speech?
gold_answer: Yes, Bell's father, grandfather, and brother were all involved in work related to elocution and speech.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
paraphrased_instruction: In what ways did the personal experiences of Alexander Graham Bell impact his professional achievements?
gold_answer: Bell's mother and wife were deaf, which profoundly influenced his research on hearing and speech.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
paraphrased_instruction: Which corporation was co-founded by Alexander Graham Bell and in what year?
gold_answer: Alexander Graham Bell co-founded the American Telephone and Telegraph Company (AT&T) in 1885.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
paraphrased_instruction: What were Alexander Graham Bell's reasons for thinking of the telephone as a disturbance?
gold_answer: Bell considered the telephone an intrusion on his real work as a scientist and refused to have one in his study.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
paraphrased_instruction: In addition to his work on the telephone, in what other fields did Alexander Graham Bell make contributions?
gold_answer: Bell also worked on optical telecommunications, hydrofoils, and aeronautics.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
paraphrased_instruction: What was Alexander Graham Bell's position or contribution to the National Geographic Society?
gold_answer: Alexander Graham Bell served as the second president of the National Geographic Society from January 7, 1898, to 1903.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
paraphrased_instruction: What is the birth year of Friedrich Nietzsche?
gold_answer: Friedrich Nietzsche was born on October 15, 1844.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
paraphrased_instruction: What country was Friedrich Nietzsche a citizen of?
gold_answer: Friedrich Nietzsche was German.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
paraphrased_instruction: How old was Friedrich Nietzsche when he was appointed as the Chair of Classical Philology at the University of Basel?
gold_answer: He became the Chair at the age of 24.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
paraphrased_instruction: What was the reason for Nietzsche's resignation from his role at the University of Basel?
gold_answer: Nietzsche resigned in 1879 due to health problems that plagued him most of his life.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
paraphrased_instruction: What significant health issue did Friedrich Nietzsche face in the year 1889?
gold_answer: In 1889, Nietzsche suffered a collapse and afterward a complete loss of his mental faculties, leading to paralysis and probably vascular dementia.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
paraphrased_instruction: What was Friedrich Nietzsche's life like in his last years?
gold_answer: He lived under the care of his mother until her death in 1897 and then with his sister Elisabeth Förster-Nietzsche until his own death in 1900.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
paraphrased_instruction: What year did Friedrich Nietzsche pass away?
gold_answer: Friedrich Nietzsche died on August 25, 1900.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
paraphrased_instruction: What are some key themes found in the philosophical writings of Friedrich Nietzsche?
gold_answer: His themes include the critique of truth and morality, the notion of the "will to power," the theory of the Übermensch, and the concept of eternal return.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
paraphrased_instruction: How does the philosophy of Friedrich Nietzsche define the idea of 'Übermensch'?
gold_answer: The Übermensch is a concept in Nietzsche's philosophy representing an individual who has overcome established values to create new ones, embodying the evolution beyond human morality.

name: Friedrich Nietzsche
paraphrased_instruction: In Nietzsche's philosophy, what does the term 'will to power' represent?
gold_answer: The "will to power" describes a fundamental drive present in individuals to assert and enhance their power and influence.

name: Edward VI of England
paraphrased_instruction: What is the birth year of England's Edward VI?
gold_answer: Edward VI of England was born on 12 October 1537.

name: Edward VI of England
paraphrased_instruction: At what point did Edward VI ascend to the throne of England?
gold_answer: Edward VI of England became king on 28 January 1547.

name: Edward VI of England
paraphrased_instruction: At what age did Edward VI of England ascend to the throne?
gold_answer: He was crowned at the age of nine.

name: Edward VI of England
paraphrased_instruction: Who was Edward VI of England's mother?
gold_answer: His mother was Jane Seymour, the third wife of Henry VIII.

name: Edward VI of England
paraphrased_instruction: Did Edward VI of England grow up following Protestant or Catholic beliefs?
gold_answer: Edward VI was the first English monarch to be raised as a Protestant.

name: Edward VI of England
paraphrased_instruction: Who was in charge of the regency council while Edward VI was king?
gold_answer: The regency council was first led by his uncle, Edward Seymour, 1st Duke of Somerset, and later by John Dudley, 1st Duke of Northumberland.

name: Edward VI of England
paraphrased_instruction: What major religious transformation took place under the rule of Edward VI of England?
gold_answer: The Church of England was transformed into a recognisably Protestant body.

name: Edward VI of England
paraphrased_instruction: What were some of the changes implemented by the Protestants during the time when Edward VI was the ruler of England?
gold_answer: Reforms included the abolition of clerical celibacy and the Mass, and the imposition of compulsory English in church services.

name: Edward VI of England
paraphrased_instruction: What were the significant social problems that arose during the rule of Edward VI of England?
gold_answer: His reign was marked by economic problems and social unrest, which erupted into riot and rebellion in 1549.

name: Edward VI of England
paraphrased_instruction: In which military conflict did Edward VI of England participate?
gold_answer: He was involved in an expensive war with Scotland.

name: Edward Elgar
paraphrased_instruction: What is the birth year of the composer Edward Elgar?
gold_answer: Edward Elgar was born on June 2, 1857.

name: Edward Elgar
paraphrased_instruction: What was the nationality of Edward Elgar?
gold_answer: Edward Elgar was English.

name: Edward Elgar
paraphrased_instruction: Can you list some famous works composed by Edward Elgar?
gold_answer: Edward Elgar composed works such as the Enigma Variations, Pomp and Circumstance Marches, and symphonies.

name: Edward Elgar
paraphrased_instruction: In which year did Edward Elgar become the Master of the King's Musick?
gold_answer: Edward Elgar was appointed Master of the King's Musick in 1924.

name: Edward Elgar
paraphrased_instruction: What obstacles did Edward Elgar encounter in his professional life?
gold_answer: Edward Elgar faced challenges due to his self-taught background, his Roman Catholicism in Protestant Britain, and his humble origins.

name: Edward Elgar
paraphrased_instruction: What led to some disputes over Elgar's choral piece, The Dream of Gerontius?
gold_answer: The Dream of Gerontius caused controversy due to its Roman Catholic text in Anglican Britain.

name: Edward Elgar
paraphrased_instruction: Which works by Edward Elgar gained significant popularity when he was in his fifties?
